summary: Hantavirus Outbreak Connected to Cruise Ship is Officially Over
summary_original: Hantavirus Outbreak Connected to Cruise Ship is Officially Over
summary_zh: 与邮轮相关的汉坦病毒疫情已正式结束

display_summary.zh: 报道指出，荷兰旗帜的邮轮 MV Hondius 上爆发的汉坦病毒疫情已由世界卫生组织正式宣布结束。WHO 表示，最后一名暴露者完成 42 天隔离并检测阴性后，疫情宣告结束，累计追踪超过 600 人，其中确认 13 例（12 例确诊、1 例可能）并导致 3 例死亡，所有病例均为船上乘客或船员。Tedros 透露，已在 33 个国家和地区跟进超过 650 名接触者，并启动涉及 21 国的研究以深化对该病毒的了解。官方确认疫情已结束且未出现持续的人际传播，但对疫情起始时间、具体传播路径仍存不确定性。
display_summary.en: The WHO has officially declared the hantavirus outbreak on the Dutch‑flagged cruise ship MV Hondius over after the last exposed individual completed a 42‑day quarantine and tested negative. The agency reported 13 cases (12 confirmed, 1 probable) and three deaths, with over 600 contacts tracked and more than 650 followed up in 33 countries. Tedros announced ongoing studies with 21 countries to better understand the disease. Official confirmation confirms the outbreak’s end, but details on its origin and transmission remain uncertain.

summary: Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa Receive Grave Plaques After Tragic Deaths
summary_original: Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa Receive Grave Plaques After Tragic Deaths
summary_zh: 杰克曼·格雷和贝丝·阿拉卡瓦获得悲剧死亡的纪念牌

display_summary.zh: 杰克曼·格雷和贝丝·阿拉卡瓦因与鼠疫相关的严重呼吸系统疾病而获得纪念牌。调查人员认为她在2025年2月中旬因类似流感的症状去世。
display_summary.en: Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa received memorial plaques after their tragic deaths. As previously reported by Radar, Betsy died from hantavirus pulmonary syndrome, a rare and severe respiratory illness linked to exposure to rodent droppings. Investigators believe she died around mid-February 2025 after experiencing flu-like symptoms.

summary: Hantavirus Infection Neurological Effects Uncovered
summary_original: Hantavirus Infection Neurological Effects Uncovered
summary_zh: 发现了引起神经症状的咸鼠疫感染

display_summary.zh: 咸鼠疫感染的神经症状罕见但严重。需要前瞻性研究和实验模型以更好地理解病理生理学、识别治疗目标和管理指南。
display_summary.en: Hantavirus infection's neurological effects are rare but severe. Prospective studies and experimental models are needed to better understand the pathophysiology, identify therapeutic targets, and develop management guidelines.

summary: Trial by virus – Winnipeg Free Press
summary_original: Trial by virus – Winnipeg Free Press
summary_zh: 病毒试验 - 温尼伯自由新闻

display_summary.zh: 报道指出，加拿大首席公共卫生官乔斯·赖默正面临埃博拉和汉坦病毒等新兴威胁。世界卫生组织在五月初确认了汉坦病毒的暴发，并在5月14日确认了刚果民主共和国的重大埃博拉疫情。文章还提到其他乘客出现疾病并死亡的情况，但未提供官方数据。赖默在新岗位上预计不会有太多空闲时间，且需准备应对下一场全球大流行。
display_summary.en: The article reports that Canada’s chief public health officer, Dr. Joss Reimer, is confronting emerging threats such as Ebola and hantavirus. The World Health Organization confirmed a hantavirus outbreak in early May and an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo on May 14. No official case numbers are provided, and Reimer is said to have little downtime in her new role while preparing for future pandemics.

summary: Oceanwide Expeditions CEO responds to hantavirus incident
summary_original: Oceanwide Expeditions CEO responds to hantavirus incident

display_summary.en: TODAY’s headlines on TRAVEL Extra, Ireland’s leading source of travel Information Seanad passes bill to remove Dublin Airport passenger cap Irish Travel Agents Association appoints Travel Department’s James Coughlan to Board Center Parcs Ireland reports occupancy of 99pc & revenues of €2m per week Unhedged Norwegian Air Shuttle reports higher-than-expected loss of €52.5m for quarter Shannon Airport investigate baggage system breakdown on Saturday Heathrow back below pre-pandemic numbers with 7,227,494 passengers in June Ireland’s HOTTEST summers: including the year the Liffey DRIED UP Oceanwide Expeditions CEO Rémi Bouysset has shared for the first timed three deaths and additional medical treatments following a hantavirus outbreak aboard the Hondius during a crossing from Ushuaia, Argentina, to Praia, Cape Verde. The company has coordinated with medical organisations, maritime authorities, and governments to disembark guests and crew and transfer those needing urgent care. Epidemiological testing has shown the virus has come from a source in South America. The company has implemented precautionary measures to sanitise and assess the vessel before it has returned to service for the Arctic season. Oceanwide has maintained strict pest-control and biosecurity procedures. No link has existed between the infection and the vessel’s condition or onboard operations. The World Health Organization has declared the outbreak over after the final contact has completed quarantine and tested negative. The Hondius has undergone deep cleaning in Rotterdam.
